content.headline: Bethpage Black: Long Island's Toughest Test
content.description_html:
Bethpage Black, nestled in Farmingdale, New York, is a public course that doesn't apologize for its demands. At 7,230 yards from the back tees with a par of 70, it's built to challenge even accomplished golfers. The White tees play to 6,684 yards at a 74.6 rating with a 148 slope—numbers that tell you everything you need to know about what's waiting.

This is a course that rewards precision and punishes indecision. Every shot matters here, and the design makes you earn every birdie. Hole 5 stands out as the signature hole, the kind of test that stays with you long after your round ends. Plan for a full 3 hours and 36 minutes to navigate all 18 holes.

If you're looking for a pint glass that captures the spirit of a course that doesn't flinch, this is it. Bethpage Black represents the kind of golf that separates the serious players from the casual ones.
bullets: 7,230 yards, par 70—a relentless test from the back
148 slope rating from the White tees demands respect
Signature hole 5 is the course's defining moment
Plan 3 hours 36 minutes for the full 18-hole experience
